東京 · 春 · 2026

Tokyo, April.
Ten days.

Dates Apr 3 – 12, 2026
Travelers 6 people
Base Nakano, Tokyo
Arrives 1:35pm Friday
Sabbath Sabbath: Apr 4 · Kawazu: Apr 11
Home base 6 Chome-3-10 Nakano, Nakano City, Tokyo · 中野６丁目３−１０

Day by day — click any day to expand
Group day
Sabbath / free
Flex / arrival
1
Apr 3
Fri
Arrival — Nakano check-in
Transit Broadway? Dinner
Afternoon
Land 1:35pm · Nakano · check in
Evening
Nakano Broadway if time · dinner
▾
Schedule
1:35pm
Land at Narita or Haneda. Allow 60–90 min for customs and baggage. Pick up Suica IC cards and SIM cards at the airport.
~4:00pm
Take train to Nakano. From Narita: Narita Express to Shinjuku, transfer to Chuo Line (~80 min). From Haneda: Keikyu Line to Shinjuku, Chuo Line (~50 min). Check in and drop bags.
~5:00pm
Nakano Broadway is a 5-min walk from the station. Sundown ~6:15pm — tight but doable for a quick first walk-through for the Sabbath observers. Non-observers can stay longer.
~7:00pm
Dinner along Nakano Sun Mall shotengai — the covered shopping street has izakayas, ramen, and casual spots a short walk from home base.
Notes
Keep Day 1 extremely low-key. Jet lag from New York to Tokyo (~14 hr time difference) hits hard. The goal is to land, orient, eat something real, and sleep at a reasonable hour so Day 2 isn't ruined.
Nakano Broadway closes ~8pm. If the group is too tired, skip it entirely — it's here all week.
Dinner suggestions by diet
Vegan
Vegetable ramen (ask for vegan broth — some Nakano spots offer it), or inari sushi from the konbini
Vegetarian
Agedashi tofu, vegetable tempura, tamago (egg) nigiri, kitsune udon (sweet tofu broth — check dashi)
Pescetarian
Sashimi set, salmon onigiri, kaiten-zushi conveyor belt sushi nearby
Meat eaters
Yakitori skewers at the izakaya, tonkotsu ramen, karaage chicken
Tonight's mission — three stops before bed:

1. 7-Eleven — Japan's 7-Eleven is genuinely one of the best things about the country. Not a regular convenience store. Stock up tonight for tomorrow's Sabbath picnic: onigiri (look for kombu or pickled plum for the vegan), inari sushi packs, edamame, tamagoyaki egg rolls, karaage chicken, melon bread, canned matcha or Boss coffee. Also grab tomorrow's breakfast. The quality is legitimately good — this is a highlight of the trip, not a backup plan.

2. Daiso dollar store — there's almost always one near a major station. Pick up a blue tarp (~$1 / ¥110) for the hanami picnic tomorrow morning. They also sell wet wipes, chopsticks, and small zip-lock bags (useful for phones at teamLab on Day 3).

3. Trash can note: Tokyo has almost no public trash cans — a legacy of post-1995 security measures. You will carry your wrappers and cups for a long time before finding a bin. The konbini you bought from will usually take back your trash, and parks have bins near the entrance. Pack a small bag inside your bag tomorrow as a portable trash holder. This catches everyone off guard on day one.
2
Apr 4
Shabbat
Sabbath — Asakusa kimono · shrines · Ueno hanami · JDM night (optional)
Sabbath Kimono Senso-ji Blossoms JDM optional
Morning
Asakusa · kimono pickup · Senso-ji shrines
Afternoon / evening
Ueno hanami until 4–5pm · JDM tour from Shinjuku (optional)
▾
Schedule
9:30am
Hanaka Kimono Rental — 着物レンタル華雅
Duel Asakusa IV, 4F · 2-3-7 Kaminarimon, Taito-city, Tokyo · +81-3-6231-6782

Directions from Nakano: Chuo Line → Ochanomizu, transfer to Toei Asakusa Line → A4 Exit at Asakusa Station. Walk out of A4 exit, pass the FamilyMart on your right, turn right at the corner — Hanaka is in the building above Banrai Hotel, take the elevator to 4F.

Booking at 9:30am. Present your mobile voucher — enter the venue directly with this voucher. Getting all 6 dressed takes 30–45 min. Fully paid in advance — no transactions needed on the day.

📍 View map to entrance  ·  🏢 View building photo
10:00am
Walk out dressed and go straight to Kaminarimon Gate (Thunder Gate) — the iconic red lantern shot. This is your money photo location, right outside the rental shop. Hit it before midday crowds pack in.
10:30am
Walk the full Nakamise arcade in kimono, through to the Senso-ji main hall. Wave the incense smoke over yourself for good luck. Then Hozomon inner gate and the main forecourt.
11:30am
Asakusa Jinja shrine next door — quieter than Senso-ji, beautiful stone lanterns and a torii gate. Great for photos without crowds. Then Azumabashi bridge for the Skytree-and-kimono skyline shot.
12:30pm
Lunch in Asakusa in kimono — a full experience. Then take the Ginza Line from Asakusa to Ueno (2 stops, ~5 min).
2:00pm
Ueno Park hanami picnic — lay out the tarp, unpack the konbini supplies. Cherry blossoms in the afternoon light with everyone still in kimono. Walk Shinobazu Pond after — blossom reflections in the water, Bentendo Temple on the island. No rush to leave — stay until 4–5pm and soak it in properly.
4:30pm
Return kimonos to the Asakusa shop (most allow return up to 6pm). Sabbath observers head back to Nakano for a quiet evening. Non-observers: from Asakusa, get to Shinjuku (~20 min by train) for the JDM tour pickup — see evening option below.
5:00–6:00pm — Optional JDM evening (non-observers)
Pickup from Shinjuku area. Tours run ~3–4 hours through Tokyo and Yokohama's car culture circuit — C1 inner loop highway, Rainbow Bridge, APIT Autobacs (Japan's legendary car parts megastore), and Daikoku Parking Area, the real-life Fast and Furious car meet where modified GTRs, Supras, RX-7s, and supercars gather on weekend nights. Best experienced on a Saturday night when turnout is highest — April 4 is a Saturday, perfect timing.

Book one of these options:
— Daikoku PA + Nissan Facility Tour (GetYourGuide) ↗ — includes Nissan HQ visit, Yokohama guide, ~3 hrs, meets at Shin-Yokohama Station
— Legendary JDM Underground Meetup — Tokyo pickup ↗ — C1 highway, Rainbow Bridge, APIT, Daikoku, ~4 hrs
— Ride in a GTR R35/R34 to Daikoku ↗ — same circuit but you're riding inside an actual modified Nissan GTR. Premium option.

Heads up: Daikoku PA is technically a highway rest stop — police occasionally move people on, especially on busy nights. Turnout varies. Most reviews say Saturday nights are electric; weeknights more chill. April 4 is Saturday — good odds. Tours typically return to the pickup point by 9–10pm.
Sabbath notes
Kimono rental is fully paid in advance — no transactions on the day. Everything is free: Senso-ji (free), Ueno Park (free), Nakamise window browsing only. The only spend is the IC card tap for the Ginza Line Asakusa → Ueno, which a non-observer can handle.
Best photo spots in order: Kaminarimon gate → Nakamise alley → Hozomon inner gate → main hall forecourt → Asakusa Jinja → Azumabashi bridge → Ueno Park under the blossoms.
Chill Saturday evening alternative — Akihabara car scene: If the JDM tour feels like too much after a full day, Akihabara on a Saturday evening is a low-key car spot worth knowing about. Modified builds — GTRs, Supras, kei cars — park outside Yodobashi Camera and along the main strip most weekend nights. It's free, spontaneous, no booking, and pairs well with a walk through the lit-up Akihabara electronics district and a late ramen. Zero pressure, genuinely cool.
Trash can reminder: Pack your konbini picnic items in a bag with a small empty bag inside for rubbish. Ueno Park has bins near the main entrance and food stall areas, but they fill up fast on peak blossom days. Don't count on finding one mid-park.
Food on the day
Picnic (all)
Prep the night before from konbini: onigiri (kombu or pickled plum = vegan), edamame, inari sushi, tamagoyaki for veg, karaage for meat eaters
Lunch (vegan)
Itosho near Senso-ji does kaiseki-style tofu cuisine — fully plant-forward and stunning
Lunch (veg)
Vegetable tempura sets, kitsune udon (ask about dashi), tamago sushi
Lunch (pesc)
Sushi or sashimi set near Nakamise — many small sushi restaurants within a short walk
Lunch (meat)
Tempura set with ebi (prawn) and chicken, gyudon beef bowl, yakitori near Hoppy Street
Hanaka confirmed ✓ — Fully paid at $15 pp. Pickup at 9am, 4F Duel Asakusa IV. Return kimonos by 6pm to the same location. Contact: [email protected] · +81-3-6231-6782
3
Apr 5
Sun
Localized shopping — Broadway · cameras · Harajuku sneakers · Donki
Shopping Broadway Sneakers Loose day
Afternoon
Broadway 1pm · cameras + Book Off Shinjuku
Evening
Harajuku sneaker run · Donki · Akihabara optional
▾
Schedule
1:00pm
Nakano Broadway — 5-min walk from home base. Four floors of anime figures, vintage manga, Mandarake used goods, retro games, trading cards. Cash preferred. This is the slow morning activity — browse at your own pace. Group can split here naturally.
2:30pm
Chuo Line one stop to Shinjuku. Map Camera — 1-12-5 Nishi-Shinjuku, on Camera Street. New and used gear, Japanese-market lenses and bodies, excellent English-speaking staff. One of the best camera shops in the world. Budget 30–40 min.
3:15pm
Book Off Shinjuku — a short walk from Map Camera. Japan's biggest used media chain. Cheap used manga volumes, retro games, Japanese-only releases, anime, CDs, vintage clothing on upper floors. Good browse for everyone — you can find rare stuff for almost nothing. Budget 30–40 min.
3:50pm
Quick lunch in Shinjuku — conveyor sushi, ramen, or konbini grab. Keep it fast, you want to be in Harajuku by 4:30.
4:30pm
Asics Flagship Harajuku — 1F Jingumae Tower Bldg, 1-5-8 Jingumae (Takeshita Street intersection). Japan's largest Asics store, carries Asics and AsicsTiger. Japan-exclusive colorways, foot measurement tech, English staff. Tax-free shopping with passport. Budget 45–60 min.
5:30pm
Kicks Lab — both locations, 3 min apart.
· Harajuku store: 1F San-M Bldg, 4-32-4 Jingumae — non-Nike brands, domestic retail
· Pino Harajuku store: 4-28-18 Jingumae — Nike focus, overseas exclusives
Closes 7:30pm Sunday — don't linger too long at Asics. Budget 45–60 min for both.
6:30pm
Onitsuka Tiger Yellow Concept Store — 6-3-9 Jingumae, Omotesando-dori. Four floors, opened April 2025. Full lineup — Mexico 66, Yellow Collection, DENIVITA denim, Astro Boy collabs. Japan-only models on 3F. Closes 8pm — you have 90 min, plenty of time. Tax-free with passport.
7:45pm
Uniqlo Harajuku — short walk from Omotesando. Japan-only UT graphic tees, Uniqlo U collection, basics. Fast and organized. Budget 20–30 min.
8:30pm
Don Quijote (Donki) Shinjuku — back on the Chuo Line, 1-16-5 Kabukicho. Open 24hrs. Multi-floor sensory overload — snacks, electronics, cosmetics, collectibles, duty-free. Tax refund counter if you've been accumulating receipts all day. Good group activity — everyone finds something.
10:00pm+
Akihabara — optional. Yamanote from Shinjuku (~20 min). Electronics open until 10pm, Mandarake and Super Potato for late night retro hunting. Car scene outside Yodobashi Camera on Sunday evenings. Only if the energy is there — home to Nakano is also a perfectly fine call.
Store details + tips
Nakano Broadway
Open 10am · Free entry · Cash preferred at most stalls · Mandarake (used anime) is the must-hit — multiple floors with different genres per floor
Map Camera — Shinjuku
1-12-5 Nishi-Shinjuku · Used and new, gear from Japanese market only · One of the best camera shops in the world · Staff speak English · Good for film cameras too
Book Off — Shinjuku
Short walk from Map Camera · Used manga, anime, retro games, CDs, vintage clothing · Prices are very low — great for browsing without a plan · Open Sun from ~10–11am
Asics Flagship Harajuku
1F Jingumae Tower Bldg, 1-5-8 Jingumae · Tax-free with passport · Japan exclusives marked clearly · Foot measurement service available · Opens 10am Sun
Kicks Lab
Opens 12pm Sunday · No advance booking · Both stores worth visiting for different inventories · Limited editions sell fast — go early in the afternoon
Onitsuka Tiger Yellow Concept Store
6-3-9 Jingumae, Omotesando-dori · Open 11am–8pm · Tax-free shopping · Carries Japan-only models alongside the full Yellow Collection · The 3F has heritage items not available elsewhere
Don Quijote Shinjuku
1-16-5 Kabukicho · Open 24hrs · Tax-free counter in store · The snack floor and cosmetics floor are the best value stops · Basement has the best selection of Japanese food souvenirs
Naritaya (custom watch) — Akihabara
If anyone wants to build a custom Seiko or Orient — dial, hands, case, strap, assembled in-store — Akihabara evening is the time. ~$100–300 depending on parts. Walk-in friendly. naritaya.jp ↗
Food along the route
Vegan
AIN SOPH Journey near Shinjuku — fully vegan Japanese restaurant, worth a dinner stop before or after Donki. Or konbini from Donki itself — Donki's food section has solid plant-based options
Vegetarian
Harajuku crepes on Takeshita Street (custard, fruit, matcha — all veg-friendly) for a mid-afternoon snack. Omotesando café sets for a sit-down lunch
Pescetarian
Conveyor belt sushi near Shinjuku for lunch, or Akihabara for dinner if you end up there — both areas have solid kaiten-zushi options
Meat eaters
Ichiran Ramen Shinjuku or Akihabara (solo booth, always good), yakiniku near Kabukicho for a group dinner after Donki
Pace note: This is a loose day — not everyone has to do everything. Split up naturally: some people want Broadway in depth, others want to go straight to Harajuku. Regroup at Kicks Lab around 2:30pm and go from there together. Donki at the end is a good group activity since there's something for everyone. Akihabara is optional — add it only if the energy is there.
4
Apr 6
Mon
Tokyo Disneyland — all day
Disney
Morning
Early entry · Fantasyland · Tomorrowland
Evening
Parades · night shows · late dinner
▾
Schedule
7:30am
Leave Nakano early — Disneyland is in Urayasu (Chiba), about 45 min by train. Take the JR Chuo Line to Tokyo Station, then the JR Keiyō Line to Maihama Station. Be at the gate by rope drop (8am).
8:00am
Rope drop — go straight to the most popular rides first before queues build. Pooh's Hunny Hunt, Haunted Mansion, Space Mountain. Use the Tokyo Disney Resort app for real-time wait times and mobile food ordering.
12:00pm
Lunch — pre-order via the app to skip food queues. April is busy; walk-up waits at restaurants can be 40+ min.
3:00pm
Afternoon parade — Dreaming Up! runs daily. Find a spot on the route 30 min early. One of the best Disney parades in the world.
Evening
Stay for the night show and electrical parade if energy allows. Park closes 9–10pm in April. Monday is one of the quieter park days — shorter queues than weekends.
Top 10 — plan for the day
🎯 Rope drop priorities (go immediately):
1. Pooh's Hunny Hunt — no tracks, cars move unpredictably, unlike any ride anywhere. Queue builds fast.
2. Haunted Mansion — Tokyo version has unique scenes not in US parks. One of the best in the world.
3. Space Mountain — classic, always long queues, do it early.
🎢 Mid-morning (before lunch):
4. Big Thunder Mountain — fast-moving queue, great for the group.
5. Splash Mountain — if April weather allows. Gets you wet so plan accordingly.
6. Star Tours — Star Wars simulator, randomized each ride so no two are the same.
🎯 Afternoon (after parade):
7. Buzz Lightyear's Astro Blasters — shooter ride, group competes for high score. Genuinely fun for adults.
8. It's a Small World — Tokyo version has a Japan section at the end that hits different. Worth it.
9. Dreaming Up! Parade (3pm) — not a ride but don't miss it. Find a spot 30 min early.
10. Electrical Parade Dreamlights (evening) — stay late, this is the reward. One of the best things in the park.
App strategy: Use Standby Plus on Pooh, Haunted Mansion, and Space Mountain — worth paying. Pre-order lunch through the app to skip 40-min food queues. Check wait times in real time before walking anywhere.
Rain contingency — swap with Day 5
Check the weather forecast for Monday Apr 6 a few days before. If heavy rain is forecast: swap Disney to Tuesday Apr 7 and move Mario Kart to Monday evening instead. Karting in light rain is fine — you're helmeted up. Disney in heavy rain means cancelled parades and outdoor queues. Only swap if it looks consistently heavy all day.
Food at Disneyland
Vegan
Dedicated allergy/dietary request counter near the park entrance — visit first thing to get a vegan-safe options list for that day
Vegetarian
Plaza Pavilion Restaurant has vegetable curry. Eastside Café has pasta options. Allergy counter confirms what's safe daily
Pescetarian
Shrimp and seafood at several restaurants — Magellan's (table service) has a reliable pescetarian-friendly menu
Meat eaters
Turkey legs near the castle, burgers at Tomorrowland Terrace, smoked chicken at Hungry Bear Restaurant
After Disney — late dinner (all)
Dontei Nakano — shabu shabu all-you-can-eat, steps from home base, open until 11pm. You just translated their menu — split pot with kombu dashi on one side for the vegan, spicy or sukiyaki broth on the other. Everyone sits, cooks at their own pace, no decisions needed. Perfect end to a Disney day. ☎ +81 50-1809-3236
End of day plan: Train back from Maihama to Nakano (~45 min). Head straight to Dontei — no detours, no decisions. Sit down, order the broth split, let the hot pot do the work. Everyone's exhausted, everyone eats what they want, nobody has to think. Open until 11pm so no rush leaving the park.
5
Apr 7
Tue
Shimokitazawa · Meiji Jingu · Yoyogi · Donki · Karts · Shibuya night
Shimokitazawa Meiji Jingu Street kart Shibuya night
Morning
Free — no plans until afternoon
Afternoon / night
Shibuya · karts · Scramble · dinner
▾
Tuesday Apr 7
Whatever you want — it's your morning.
Karts are at 4pm. Be in Shibuya by 3:30pm. Everything before that is yours.
⚠️ DO NOT FORGET YOUR INTERNATIONAL DRIVER'S LICENSE.
No IDP = no drive, no refund. Every single driver needs it on the day.
Street Kart Shibuya
15-3-3 Maruyamacho, Shibuya
3 min walk from Shinsen Station
15 min walk from Shibuya Station
Open in Maps ↗
Afternoon plan
Suggested morning if you want it: Shimokitazawa (10 min from Nakano) → walk east through Meiji Jingu → Yoyogi Park → Shinjuku. About an hour of walking total, all flat, all good in April.
~1:00pm
Head toward Shinjuku. Don Quijote Kabukicho — snacks, collectibles, duty-free, tax refund counter. Open 24hrs, quieter in the afternoon.
~2:00pm
Yamanote Line Shinjuku → Shibuya (2 stops, 4 min). Arrive with time to spare.
~2:15pm
Mizuno Shop Shibuya PARCO — Japan Sports Style store, exclusives, tax-free with passport.
Side mission — JINS Shibuya (glasses for mom)
Japanese eyewear chain, clean minimalist frames, ~¥5,000–10,000 all in. Budget 20–30 min. Bring her prescription if you have it.
4:00pm / 4:30pm
Mario Kart — Group 1 departs 4pm, Group 2 departs 4:30pm. Both in Shibuya. Helmet, gloves, costume. Driver's license required. ~1 hour on the road.
5:30pm onwards
Shibuya at night — regroup, Scramble Crossing lit up, dinner, wander. Stay as long as the energy holds. No early morning tomorrow.
Food on the day
Lunch — vegan / veg
Shimokitazawa indie cafés — several vegetarian-friendly spots in the backstreets. Ask staff, most boards have veg options marked
Lunch — meat / pesc
Shimokitazawa ramen (Zund Bar is a local favourite) or curry in the backstreets
Dinner — vegan
AIN SOPH Journey is near Shibuya — fully vegan Japanese restaurant, worth booking ahead for the group
Dinner — all
Shibuya has everything. Genki Sushi (conveyor + touchscreen ordering, fast and fun), yakiniku for a group grill, or just wander Dogenzaka for whatever looks good
Late night
Ichiran Ramen Shibuya — 24hr, solo booth experience, vegan broth on request. Perfect end to a long night
The arc: Shimokitazawa → Meiji Jingu → Yoyogi Park → Shinjuku → Shibuya is one clean eastward line with no backtracking. The walking portion between Shimokitazawa and Shinjuku is about an hour total — comfortable, flat, and passes through some of the most interesting residential parts of the city. Save energy for Shibuya at night — that's the main event.
6
Apr 8
Wed
Mt. Fuji day trip — all day
Mt. Fuji Nature Blossoms Day trip
Morning
Bus departs ~8am · Kawaguchiko
Afternoon
Lake walk · blossoms · return ~6–7pm
▾
Schedule
7:00am
Early breakfast and pack a bag — layers, sunscreen, snacks, water. It's colder near Fuji even in April (~5–10°C at the 5th Station if you go up).
8:00am
Bus departs from Shinjuku Bus Terminal. Journey to Kawaguchiko is ~2 hours. Wednesday is a weekday — significantly fewer tourists than weekends at the viewpoints.
10:00am
Arrive at Lake Kawaguchi. In early April, Kawaguchiko cherry blossoms are often still at or near peak — with Fuji as a backdrop this is one of Japan's most photographed scenes. Walk the north shore for the best Fuji reflection shots.
12:00pm
Lunch at a lakeside restaurant. Hoto noodle soup — thick flat noodles in miso broth with vegetables, the regional specialty. Vegetarian-friendly versions available.
1:30pm
Fuji 5th Station (if included in tour, ~2,300m elevation) for above-cloud views on clear days. Or Oshino Hakkai — a village of 8 sacred spring ponds fed by Fuji snowmelt, extremely photogenic.
~6:00pm
Return to Shinjuku. Wednesday means no Sabbath timing concern — relax and take the evening as it comes.
Booking notes
Recommended operators: Klook, Viator, or Hato Bus. Look for tours departing by 8am. Cost ~$53–80 pp (¥8,000–12,000). Wednesday availability is usually good — book 1–2 weeks out.
Mt. Fuji is not climbable in April — climbing season opens July 1. The day trip is about the views, the lake, and the surrounding area. Visibility is the main variable — Apr 8 is historically a clearer week in spring.
Food on the day
All diets
Hoto noodles — vegetarian miso broth version available at most lakeside restaurants. The regional comfort food after a day outdoors
Vegan
Pack konbini supplies from Nakano the night before — rural Yamanashi has limited vegan options. Inari sushi, kombu onigiri, fruit
Meat / pesc
Grilled river fish (ayu) at lakeside restaurants — local spring specialty
7
Apr 9
Thu
Gap day — free day 1
Free day 1
Morning
Slow start · rest after Disney
Afternoon
Free — solo or small groups
▾
Thursday Apr 9
Whatever you want — it's your day.
Ideas if you want them
Shimokitazawa — bohemian neighbourhood 10 min from Nakano. Vintage clothing, independent record shops, live music venues, low-key cafés. Best browsed slowly with no agenda.
Yanaka — old Tokyo neighbourhood that survived the war. Narrow streets, family-run shops, cats, a historic cemetery lined with cherry trees. Very quiet and atmospheric.
Shinjuku Gyoen — one of Tokyo's best parks, $3 entry (¥500). Peak cherry blossoms may still be going. Large enough to sit and read for hours.
Koenji — neighbourhood adjacent to Nakano. Alternative culture, vintage stores, good ramen, no tourists. Worth an afternoon wander.
Tokyo National Museum — Ueno. World's largest collection of Japanese art. Full day if interested.
Nakano Broadway — revisit if you didn't get enough on Day 3. Or just sit in a café near home and do nothing. That's valid too.
Good food near Nakano on a slow day
All diets
Nakano Sun Mall has a mix of everything — ramen, soba, curry, conveyor sushi. Good for a casual group lunch without committing to a neighbourhood
Vegan / veg
Shimokitazawa has several vegetarian-friendly cafés if you head that way — Herald Café and Munch are popular local spots
Meat / pesc
Koenji has excellent yakitori alley spots and a strong ramen scene — worth the 5-min train ride
8
Apr 10
Fri
Free day 2 — no plans, your call
Free day 2
All day
Loose — solo, pairs, or group
Evening
Prep for Kawazu tomorrow · early night
▾
Friday Apr 10
Whatever you want — it's your day.
Ideas + tomorrow's prep
Akihabara — electronics, anime, retro games, arcades. Yodobashi Camera alone is 8 floors. Good on a Friday when it's less chaotic than weekends.
Yanaka — old Tokyo that survived the war. Narrow streets, cats, temple cemetery lined with late cherry trees, family-run shops. Quiet and atmospheric. 30 min from Nakano.
Koenji — next stop from Nakano on the Chuo Line. Alternative culture, vintage shops, strong ramen scene, zero tourists.
Nothing — also a valid answer. A quiet Friday near the accommodation, good food, early night is not wasted time.
Tomorrow — Kawazu prep: Confirm everyone has grip shoes for the waterfall trail (slippery stone steps). Pack a swimsuit and small towel. Stock konbini snacks for the train — Kawazu area has limited vegan options. Odoriko departs Shinjuku in the morning — check your confirmation for exact time.
Food near Nakano — low effort Friday
All diets
Nakano Sun Mall — ramen, soba, conveyor sushi, curry. Everything within 5-min walk, no planning required
Vegan / veg
Shimokitazawa cafés if you feel like a short trip — or konbini bowls and inari sushi for a fully no-effort day
Meat / pesc
Nakano yakitori alleys at night — smoky, cheap, local. The best version of this neighbourhood
9
Apr 11
Sat
Odoriko → Kawazu Seven Waterfalls → onsen
Waterfalls Scenic train Onsen Izu spring
Morning
Odoriko from Shinjuku · scenic coast
Afternoon
Seven Waterfalls hike · Amagiso onsen
▾
Schedule
9:30am
Head to Shinjuku Station. On Saturdays the Odoriko departs from Shinjuku — perfect since you're based in Nakano (5 min on the Chuo Line). Grab breakfast at the station or a konbini on the way.
~10:00am
Board the Odoriko limited express. A comfortable scenic train that hugs the Pacific coastline from Atami onward — ocean views on the right side heading south. On clear days you may catch a glimpse of Mt. Fuji. Book reserved seats for all 6 in the same car. Journey to Kawazu is ~2.5 hours.
~12:30pm
Arrive at Kawazu Station. Take the Tokai Bus C50 toward Shuzenji (~25 min, ~$5 / ¥730) to the Kawazu Nanadaru Onsen stop at the trailhead.
1:00–3:00pm
Walk the Kawazu Seven Waterfalls trail — 1.3km one-way through a forested gorge, seven distinct waterfalls each named and featuring a Lucky God statue. The trail ends at Odaru Falls, the largest at 30m. Wear shoes with good grip — the mossy stone steps are slippery when wet. Allow 2 hours at a relaxed pace.
3:00–5:00pm
Amagiso Odaru Onsen at the base of Odaru Falls — one of the most unique onsen in Japan. Mixed-gender open-air baths right at the foot of a 30m waterfall. Swimsuits required (bring your own or rent at reception). Completely tattoo-friendly — no restrictions, no stickers needed. Multiple rotenburo (outdoor baths) along the river, a hot spring lap pool downstream, and a hidden cave onsen to explore. Entry ~$7 pp (¥1,000).
~5:30pm
Bus back to Kawazu Station. Optional: walk the wasabi fields just past Odaru Falls before leaving — fresh wasabi growing in clear mountain stream water, one of the most unusual sights in Japan.
~6:00pm
Board the return Odoriko back to Tokyo (~2.5 hrs). Arrive Shinjuku ~8:30pm. Final group dinner near Nakano or Shinjuku — the last big night.
Train + booking
Odoriko — book in advance:
Saturday departures leave from Shinjuku. Reserved seats are required — the train runs once or twice daily and fills up, especially in spring. Book as early as possible.

— Book via Klook ↗ (search Tokyo to Kawazu, Odoriko)
— Or book directly at any JR Ticket Office or via the JR East reservation website

Cost: ~$19–30 pp one-way depending on seat class. Reserved ordinary seats are fine for 6 — just book together in the same car.
Amagiso Odaru Onsen: ~$7 pp (¥1,000) entry. Bring your swimsuit — no nude bathing in the outdoor baths. Towel rental available (~$1 / ¥100). Open Thursday–Tuesday, 10am–4pm. Closed Wednesdays. No booking needed — walk in. Note: the path down to the onsen is steep and slippery. Wear appropriate footwear for the trail.
Total trip cost estimate per person: Train round trip ~$50–130 + bus ~$10 + onsen ~$7 = roughly $67–147 pp for the whole day. One of the most unique days on the itinerary and one of the cheapest.
Food on the day
On the train
Pick up bento boxes and snacks from a konbini or the station before boarding — Shinjuku Station has excellent ekiben (station bento) options on the concourse. Eat on the train and enjoy the coastal views
Vegan / veg
Pack konbini supplies from Nakano the night before — the Kawazu trail area has limited vegan options. Inari sushi, onigiri (kombu filling), and fruit are reliable. The wasabi fields sell fresh wasabi products including wasabi-flavored snacks
Pesc / meat
Kawazu town near the station has seafood restaurants and soba spots — good for a quick lunch before the bus. Grilled river fish (ayu) is the local specialty in spring
Final dinner (all)
Back in Shinjuku or Nakano — make it a proper last-night izakaya. Wagyu yakiniku works for the whole group: meat eaters get wagyu, vegans/veg get mushrooms and vegetables on the grill, everyone shares the table
Why this day works: The Odoriko is a classic scenic express — Pacific coastline, possible Fuji views, easy reserved seating. The waterfalls trail is one hour of relaxed forest walking. And Amagiso is one of the only truly tattoo-friendly, mixed-gender, open-air waterfall onsens in Japan — genuinely rare. For ~$7 entry next to a 30m waterfall in a swimsuit, it's one of the most memorable things you'll do on the trip.
10
Apr 12
Sun
Departure morning
Tsukiji Depart
Morning
Tsukiji breakfast · konbini run · pack
Midday
Airport transfer · depart Tokyo
▾
Schedule
7:00am
Final konbini run — stock up on omiyage (souvenir snacks) for the flight. Kit Kat matcha, Pocky, regional wagashi. Last chance.
8:00am
Tsukiji Outer Market for breakfast — the inner market moved to Toyosu but the outer market remains. Tamagoyaki (egg roll) fresh off the grill, sushi for breakfast, fruit on skewers. Budget 1.5 hours.
10:00am
Return to Nakano to collect bags. Check out of accommodation.
11:00am
Airport transfer. From Nakano: Chuo Line to Shinjuku, Narita Express to Narita (~85 min, $21 / ¥3,070). For Haneda: Chuo Line to Shinjuku, Keio Line to Haneda (~40 min). Allow 3 hours before international departure.
Final notes
Tsukiji is a 25-min train ride from Nakano — take the Marunouchi Line from Nakano-Sakaue to Tsukiji (4 stops). Leave bags at the accommodation during breakfast, collect on the way to the airport.
The airport has IC card refund machines at most terminals — cash out remaining Suica balances before departure (minus the $3 (¥500) deposit which is refunded separately).
Tsukiji breakfast by diet
Vegan
Tsukiji is seafood-heavy — best picks: fresh fruit stalls, tamagoyaki is egg-only (veg not vegan), pickled vegetable stalls, and sesame-coated rice crackers
Vegetarian
Tamagoyaki (egg roll) fresh off the grill — a Tsukiji classic. Several stalls sell it on a stick, hot. Also: tamago sushi, fruit
Pescetarian
Sushi for breakfast — yes, really. Freshest possible fish, counter seats, order omakase for ~$13–20 (¥2,000–3,000). This is the move
Meat eaters
Wagyu beef skewer stalls are present alongside the seafood — find them in the inner market alley
Safe travels. Don't forget to tap out of the IC card system at the airport — and check every pocket for yen coins before you board. Japanese vending machines are generous.
02
Food guide — eating as a mixed group
Strategy
Go for izakaya
Izakayas serve dozens of small shared plates — yakitori for meat eaters, agedashi tofu and grilled veg for vegetarians, sashimi for the pescetarian. One table, everyone's covered. Look for ones with a photo menu.
Watch out for
Hidden dashi
Traditional Japanese cooking uses dashi (fish stock) as a base in miso soup, noodle broths, and sauces. Dishes that look vegetarian often aren't. Ask: 「だしは何ですか？」 (What is the dashi made from?)
For vegans + vegetarians
Safe bets
Inari sushi (tofu pockets), onigiri with kombu or pickled plum filling, zaru soba, vegetable tempura at vegan-specific spots, and T's TanTan in Tokyo Station — fully vegan ramen.
For the pescetarian
Spoiled for choice
Conveyor belt sushi (kaiten-zushi), kaisendon sashimi bowls, grilled fish teishoku set meals, prawn tempura. Toyosu Market on Day 3 is the highlight — fresh uni, tuna, salmon at the source.
For meat eaters
Don't miss
Wagyu yakiniku, tonkatsu, tonkotsu ramen, yakitori in Omoide Yokocho. Nakano has excellent yakitori alleys 5 min from the accommodation.
Must-try for everyone
Konbini culture
7-Eleven, FamilyMart, and Lawson are a food experience. Onigiri, egg salad sandwiches, melon bread, cold matcha. Budget one full konbini breakfast as a group activity — it's part of the trip.
03
Book before you land
Kimono rental — Asakusa · Apr 4
Booked at ~$15 pp — great find. Fully paid before sundown Friday Apr 3. Pick up and get dressed at the Asakusa shop Saturday morning, do shrine photos in Asakusa, then travel to Ueno in kimono for the afternoon picnic.
✓ Confirmed
Tokyo Disneyland — Apr 6 (Monday)
Booked. Arrive at rope drop (8am). Download the Tokyo Disney Resort app for wait times and mobile food ordering. Check the rain forecast for Monday Apr 6 a few days before — see Day 4 rain contingency note.
✓ Confirmed
Mt. Fuji bus tour — Apr 8 (Wednesday)
Booked. Departs from Shinjuku. Bring layers — it's colder near Fuji even in April. Pack konbini snacks the night before, especially for the vegan in the group.
✓ Confirmed
Odoriko train — Apr 11 (Saturday)
Booked — reserved seats for all 6. Saturday departure from Shinjuku Station. Check confirmation for exact departure time and platform.
✓ Confirmed
Mario Kart — Apr 7 (Tuesday)
Booked — ~4pm slot. All drivers need a valid license on the day. Shimokitazawa in the morning leaves plenty of time to get there. After kart, Golden Gai or Omoide Yokocho in Shinjuku for the evening.
✓ Confirmed
04
Weather forecast — Tokyo, April 3–12

Live forecast via Open-Meteo. Accuracy improves within 7 days of your trip — check back closer to departure.

✦
Your days — personal itinerary

Free days are yours. These are ideas — not obligations. Mix, drop, or swap as you feel on the day.

Day 7 · Thursday Apr 9
Dog Heart · Daigo · decompress
Morning — Dog Heart
Dog Heart — 2F Y's Park Building, 1-45-2 Tomigaya, Shibuya (near Yoyogi Park). Toy poodles, beagles, golden retrievers. Hang inside with all of them, or rent one and take it into Yoyogi Park for a walk.

Inside playtime: ~$9 / ¥1,300 for 30 min. Dog rental: ~$24 / ¥3,600 for 1 hour — you get the dog, a leash, bag, treats. Open daily 11am–7pm. Call ahead to reserve a specific dog.

Get there around 10:30–11am, spend an hour or two, walk in Yoyogi after. Then head toward Kamiyacho for lunch.
View on maps ↗
Lunch — Daigo · Michelin · shojin ryori
Daigo — 2-3-1 Atago, Minato-ku (Forest Tower 2F). The only shojin ryori restaurant in the world to hold Michelin stars. 70+ years of Buddhist temple cuisine, multi-course kaiseki, private dining rooms with views of a moss garden. One of the best meals you'll have anywhere.

Important: The standard menu uses bonito dashi in the broth — not fully vegetarian by default. When booking, explicitly request the fully vegetarian/vegan course with no animal products. They accommodate this but need advance notice.

Lunch: ~$125–$254 pp. Last entry 1pm — book the earliest available slot. Meal runs 2–2.5 hours. 5-min walk from Kamiyacho Station (Hibiya Line).
Michelin Guide ↗ Reserve via Tableall ↗ Reserve via byFood ↗
Afternoon — decompress
After a meal like that you won't want to rush anywhere. Daigo is right next to Atago Hill and Shiba Park — both quiet, worth a slow walk. Or head back to Nakano and rest. Keep the evening loose — Kawazu is Saturday.
Late night (optional) — batting cages
Batter's Box Shinjuku — multiple lanes, different pitch speeds, open until ~11pm. Two stops from Nakano on the Chuo Line. Token-based, easy to just show up and go. Perfect after a slow day — active enough to be fun, no commitment, done when you feel like it.

Alternatively there are arcade-style batting centers in Shibuya with a game-center vibe — scoreboards, tokens, competitive if you want it to be. Either works as a 9–10pm activity.
Book Daigo now. Reservations are taken weeks to months in advance, especially for April spring season. Don't wait on this one.
Day 8 · Friday Apr 10
Hamarikyu Tea House + massage day
Morning — Hamarikyu Gardens + tea house
Hamarikyu Gardens — a 400-year-old tidal garden right in central Tokyo, surrounded by skyscrapers. Entry ¥300 (~$2). Walk the grounds, then head to Nakajima-no-Ochaya — the tea house on a small island in the middle of the pond, connected by wooden bridges. Sit on tatami mats with your shoes off, matcha and a wagashi sweet, views of the water and ducks. Very peaceful. No reservation — just show up. Matcha + sweet ~$7 / ¥1,000.

Open 9am–4:30pm. Get there before noon when it fills up.
View on maps ↗
Afternoon — massage
Hamarikyu is near Ginza and Tsukiji — both have excellent massage spots. Look for a Thai massage or anma (Japanese pressure massage) parlour in the area. Budget ~$30–50 for 60–90 min. These are walk-in friendly in Tokyo — no booking usually needed on a weekday afternoon.

Alternatively: Thermae-Yu in Shinjuku (~20 min away) is a non-nude onsen with a relaxation floor and massage options. Same idea as Manyo Club but in Shinjuku, easier to get back from. Entry ~$25, massages extra.
Evening — Tsukiji outer market or Ginza
Hamarikyu is a 10-min walk from Tsukiji outer market — the inner market moved but the outer stalls are still going. Great for a late afternoon food wander. Fresh tamagoyaki, seafood skewers, Japanese snacks. Then early night — Kawazu is tomorrow.
Pet café option: If Day 7 dog rental doesn't happen, Day 8 afternoon also works. There's also Shimokitazawa Cat Café (~15 min from Nakano) if you'd rather cats — calmer energy, good for a post-tea-house slow afternoon.
Tokyo · April 3–12, 2026 · 6 travelers · Nakano base
